features.summary: Costco reported strong Q2 fiscal 2022 results with net income of $1.299 billion ($2.92 per share) versus $951 million ($2.14 per share) in the prior year. Net sales increased 16.1% to $50.94 billion, with comparable sales up 14.4% reported and 11.1% excluding gas inflation. Membership fee income grew 9.8% to $967 million, with renewal rates reaching record highs of 92.0% in the U.S./Canada and 89.6% worldwide. Executive membership penetration increased to 42.7% of members representing 70.9% of sales.
features.management_outlook: Management expects continued inflationary pressures with overall price inflation estimated at 6% in Q2, up from 4.5-5% in Q1. The company plans 32 new warehouse openings for fiscal 2022 (28 net new after relocations) with capital expenditures of approximately $4 billion. Management remains confident in their competitive position and ability to drive sales through value proposition, while maintaining their methodical approach to international expansion with expectations of 25-30 annual openings going forward.
features.risks: Supply chain challenges persist including port delays, container shortages, COVID disruptions, component shortages, labor cost pressures, and truck driver shortages. Inflationary pressures continue across multiple categories including labor, freight, commodities, and raw materials, with suppliers indicating additional price increases coming. The company faces ongoing LIFO charges ($71 million in Q2) due to inflation, and there are potential demand destruction risks if economic conditions deteriorate significantly.
features.key_drivers: Strong membership loyalty with record renewal rates and growing executive membership conversion driving fee income growth. Robust comparable sales growth of 11.1% excluding gas inflation supported by 9.3% traffic increases worldwide. Gas business remains a significant profit driver with improved margins across the retail gas sector. E-commerce growth of 12.6% and continued expansion of Costco Logistics with 85% of U.S. less-than-truckload shipments now handled internally, supporting big and bulky sales growth.
